LanguageData.UR_SectionConsumerGoodsDesc: Consumer goods are automatically consumed from a settlement's stockpile when negative events strike, reducing their impact on happiness and unrest.
LanguageData.UR_SettingsCGConversionRate: CG conversion rate (CG per penalty point): {0}
LanguageData.UR_SectionToolsDesc: Cities produce tools that boost production at nearby rural settlements. Tools are consumed from the city's stockpile each tax tick.
LanguageData.UR_SettingsToolsCost: Tools cost per rural per tick: {0}
LanguageData.UR_UrbanNoResources: Urban settlements cannot produce raw resources. Supply routes from rural settlements are required to sustain city buildings and population.
LanguageData.UR_RequiresFlatTerrain: Requires flat or gently hilly terrain.
LanguageData.FCDescUrbanCenter: Where the wilderness once stood, roads and rooftops now stretch to the horizon. No crops grow here, no timber is felled; the land has long since given way to industry and commerce. The city thrives on the labor of its people and the goods shipped in from the surrounding countryside.
LanguageData.UR_ConsumerGoodsConsumed: {0} Consumer Goods consumed at {1} (-{2}% impact from {3})
